text: Being here, on the threshold between sterile hallways and the vibrant heart of an exhibit, I feel anticipation mixed with curiosity. The glass wall feels like a thin veil between the mundane and the thought-provoking. This border zone amplifies my sense of being an observer, neither entirely inside nor out. The angle at which I stand distorts my perception, making the familiar world feel slightly uncanny—shifting my awareness from passive recipient to active engager with the space.
